title: Prefer record pattern over explicit field access
description: When working with records, use pattern matching instead of accessing fields directly. This approach improves code clarity and leverages modern Java features for safer and more concise code. Refactoring to record patterns can reduce boilerplate and potential errors.
recommendation: Refactor code to use record pattern matching instead of explicit field access when working with records.

noncompliant:
int x = point.x();
int y = point.y();
compliant: if (point instanceof Point(int x, int y)) { ... }

checks.message: Refactor code to use record pattern matching instead of explicit field access when working with records.
